change: Refactor _run_single_cycle fallback logic (lines 527-536): when all trials are skipped due to provider_blocked, do NOT run the fallback agent.run_task(). Currently if all 3 trials get skipped via provider_blocked early termination, the code falls through to the fallback which spawns yet another CLI call that will also fail. Add a guard: if provider_blocked is True when candidates is empty, return an empty failure result instead of running the fallback.
reason: When auth is broken, the provider_blocked flag correctly skips trials 2-3, but the fallback path at line 528 still fires, wasting one more CLI call per cycle. This is the last source of wasted CLI calls during auth outages.

now: Guard fallback path in _run_single_cycle against provider_blocked
Add 'failed to shutdown rollout recorder' to auth guard patterns in _provider_auth_probe()
next: Refactor _run_once() stream parsing — extract rate-limit retry into helper to reduce 100-line duplication
Add structured failure taxonomy beyond auth/none (timeout, syntax, import, runtime)
Improve fitness scoring to weight auth failures at 0.0 instead of 0.25
later: Adaptive cooldown based on historical rate-limit patterns
Cross-session learning transfer via structured memory compression
